behavior: Agile bark-forager; climbs trunks and large branches in any orientation, often head-down, probing crevices and caching seeds.
field_marks: Compact, big-headed, almost neckless shape with a very short tail
Long, narrow, sharply pointed bill, straight to slightly upturned
Crisp white face, throat, breast, and belly
Dark cap and nape: blacker in males, grayer in females
Chestnut to rusty wash or patches on the lower belly and under-tail coverts
habitat: Mature deciduous woodland and woodland edge; also mixed forest with large trees, parks, and gardens.

sources.title: White-breasted Nuthatch Identification — All About Birds
